# Pondhawk.Logging

> A standalone .NET structured logging API by Pond Hawk Technologies on Microsoft.Extensions.Logging (method tracing, object/typed-payload logging, [Sensitive] masking) — plus a Watch Server provider (ZLogger-based, Channel batching, dynamic switch-based level control) and a journald-optimized console. Three packages, all target net8.0, no dependency on other Pondhawk packages.

## Packages

### Pondhawk.Logging — logging API

Install: `dotnet add package Pondhawk.Logging`

**Key types (`Pondhawk.Logging` namespace):**
- `LoggingExtensions` — extensions on `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger`:
  - `EnterMethod()` → disposable method-trace scope (auto entry/exit + elapsed)
  - `Inspect(name, value)` → logs `"{Name} = {Value}"` at Debug
  - `LogObject(value)` / `LogObject(title, value)` → serialize an object to a JSON payload
  - `LogJson` / `LogSql` / `LogXml` / `LogYaml` / `LogText(title, content)` → typed payloads with syntax-highlight hints
- `[Sensitive]` — masks a property when an object is serialized
- `CorrelationManager`, `PayloadType`, `JsonObjectSerializer`, `LogPropertyNames` (the `Pondhawk.*` log-state property-name contract sinks read)

Loggers come from the standard `ILoggerFactory` (`CreateLogger<T>()` / `CreateLogger(Type)` / `CreateLogger(string)`), returning `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger`.

```csharp
using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;
using Pondhawk.Logging;

using var _ = logger.EnterMethod();
logger.Inspect(nameof(orderId), orderId);
logger.LogObject("order", order);
logger.LogSql("query", sqlText);
```
**No sink or transport** — a provider (e.g. Pondhawk.Logging.Watch) supplies delivery, or an app uses any Microsoft.Extensions.Logging provider.

### Pondhawk.Logging.Watch — Watch Server provider

Install: `dotnet add package Pondhawk.Logging.Watch`
```csharp
using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;
using Pondhawk.Logging.Watch;

builder.Logging.AddWatch("http://localhost:11000", "MyApp");   // Watch Server controls levels via switches
```
**Key types (`Pondhawk.Logging.Watch` namespace):**
- `WatchLoggingBuilderExtensions` — `AddWatch(this ILoggingBuilder, serverUrl, domain, configure?)`: registers a level filter driven by the switch table and a ZLogger delivery processor
- `WatchLoggerProcessor` — a ZLogger `IAsyncLogProcessor` with unbounded `Channel<T>` batching and a circuit breaker; events serialized as MemoryPack
- `SwitchSource` / `SwitchDef` / `WatchSwitchSource` — dynamic per-category level control (longest-prefix match); `WatchSwitchSource` polls a Watch Server
- `LogEvent` / `LogEventBatch` — the wire event model

### Pondhawk.Logging.Console — journald-optimized console

Install: `dotnet add package Pondhawk.Logging.Console`
```csharp
using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;
using Pondhawk.Logging.Console;

builder.Logging.AddJournaldConsole();   // fixed at Warning; for Linux production under systemd
```
**Key types (`Pondhawk.Logging.Console` namespace):**
- `JournaldConsoleLoggingBuilderExtensions` — `AddJournaldConsole(this ILoggingBuilder)`: a ZLogger console whose plain-text formatter prefixes each line with the sd-daemon priority (`<N>` mapped from `LogLevel`) and category, with no timestamp and no ANSI color; exceptions render inline (single journald entry). Fixed at Warning.

## Docs
- [README.md](README.md) — overview and examples
- [src/Pondhawk.Logging/README.md](src/Pondhawk.Logging/README.md), [src/Pondhawk.Logging.Watch/README.md](src/Pondhawk.Logging.Watch/README.md) — NuGet package readmes
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) plus per-project `src/*/CLAUDE.md` — repository/build guidance

## Technical details
- All three packages target `net8.0`; built on Microsoft.Extensions.Logging (the Watch and Console providers use ZLogger)
- XML documentation ships inside the NuGet packages (100% public-API coverage; consumers get IntelliSense)
- `Pondhawk.Logging.Watch` is consumed by the pondhawk/watch-server client
- MIT licensed; extracted from the pondhawktech/tools monorepo
